rulings.Case Title: SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT, INC. VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Compliance And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 28, 2021, Line 3, PLAINTIFF SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT, INC.'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Compliance And Sanctions Plaintiff's motion "to compel production of documents compliance and sanctions" is denied without prejudice to filing a CCP 2024.050 motion. The trial court otherwise lacks jurisdiction to hear the motion. (Pelton-Shepherd Industries v. Delta Packaging Products (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 1568, 1585-87.) The judge pro tem's report and recommendation are adopted. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CONSTANCE RAMOS VS. WINSTON & STRAWN, LLP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Answer
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 28, 2021, Line 4, DEFENDANT WINSTON & STRAWN, LLP'S Motion For Leave To File Amended Answer Defendant's motion for leave to file an amended answer is granted, as "it is a rare case in which a court will be justified in refusing a party leave to amend" a pleading. (Board of Trustees of Leland Stanford Jr. University v. Sup. Ct. (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 1154, 1163.) With an October 4 trial date, ample time exists to demur to the new affirmative defense if a demurrer is appropriate. rulings.Case Title: TIEMING YAO VS. YANYING LIU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Tieming Yao Motion For Summary Judgment Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment (For Tentative Ruling Purposes Only, Part 2 Of 2)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 28, 2021, Line 5, Plaintiff Tieming Yao Motion For Summary Judgment (For Tentative Ruling Purposes Only, Part 2 Of 2) Second, the Chinese judgment arose after the filing of this action and plaintiff would need to file a supplemental complaint. The general rule is that a supplemental complaint cannot plead an entirely new cause of action, however. (See Flood v. Simpson (1975) 45 Cal.App.3d 644, 647 ["[T]he court properly denied the motion on the basis that the supplement to the complaint sought to introduce new causes of action."]; but see Honig v. Financial Corp. of America (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 960, 966 [permitting a defamation claim that arose after the filing of the underlying harassment action].) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: TIEMING YAO VS. YANYING LIU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 28, 2021, Line 5, PLAINTIFF TIEMING YAO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (part 1 of 2) Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment is denied. "[A] plaintiff, in order to obtain recognition of a foreign-country money judgment must proceed in accordance with the normal procedures applicable to actions and not by way of a motion or petition...." (Hyundai Sec. Co. v. Lee (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 682, 693; see also Manco Contracting Co. (W.L.L.) v. Bezdikian (2008) 45 Cal.4th 192, 207 ["California has not enacted an expedited procedure for the registration of foreign judgments. Rather, a party seeking recognition of a foreign judgment... must file a civil action."].) Plaintiff's operative complaint is the First Amended Complaint filed on June 27, 2018. That pleading makes no mention of a foreign judgment-obviously, because it did not exist at that time. Because the operative complaint does not mention the foreign judgment, this motion is procedurally defective. In other words, a plaintiff cannot obtain summary judgment on a matter not pleaded in his/her complaint. "The purpose of a summary judgment proceeding is to permit a party to show that material factual claims arising from the pleadings need not be tried because they are not in dispute. The function of the pleadings in a motion for summary judgment is to delimit the scope of the issues: the function of the affidavits or declarations is to disclose whether there is any triable issue of fact within the issues delimited by the pleadings. The complaint measures the materiality of the facts tendered in a defendant's challenge to the plaintiff's cause of action." (FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 381 (internal quotations and citations omitted).) The court concludes that plaintiff needs to file a new action for this court to recognize the judgment. First, the language of CCP 1718 supports this conclusion. (See Manco Contracting Co. (W.L.L.) v. Bezdikian (2008) 45 Cal.4th 192, 207, fn. 12.) CCP sec. 1718 provides: (a) If recognition of a foreign-country judgment is sought as an original matter, the issue of recognition shall be raised by filing an action seeking recognition of the foreign-country judgment. (b) If recognition of a foreign-country judgment is sought in a pending action, the issue of recognition may be raised by counterclaim, cross-claim, or affirmative defense. (End of tentative ruling part 1 of 2, see part 2) =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ALEXANDER ET AL VS. GURU SHARANAM, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Compliance Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, May 28, 2021, Line 5, Compliance Hearing: Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Matter continued to June 11, 2020. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY A/S/O ANG LI VS. BSH HOME APPLIANCES CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Request For Admissions And Request For Production Of Documents, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, May 28, 2021, Line 2, (Part 1 of 2 for tentative ruling) PLAINTIFF STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY A/S/O ANG LI's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Request For Admissions And Request For Production Of Documents, And Request For Sanctions: Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel further responses to Requests for Admissions #1-9, 13, 14, 18, 19, 20, 22 and 23 is Granted. All objections are overruled. The court finds that defendant BSH did not make a good faith effort to obtain information in order to properly answer the requests for admissions. See Regency Health Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 64 CA4th 1496. "A party cannot plead ignorance to information which can be obtained from sources under his control." Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 CA3d 771, 779 This includes a party's lawyer Smith v. Superior Court (1961) 189 CA2d 6, 12, agents or employees Gordon v. Sup. Ct. (1984) 161 CA 3d 151, 167-168; family members Jones v. Superior Court (1981) 119 CA 3d 534, 552 and experts who have been retained by a party. Sigerseth v. Superior Court (1972) 23 CA 3d 427, 433. Motion to compel further answers to 17.1 is granted. All objections are overruled. Facts are discoverable even if discovered by an expert. CCP2034.210 et seq. only protects expert's opinions. Motion to compel further responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One #7, 9, 18, 19, and 38-40 are granted. All objections are overruled. The court finds that defendant BSH did not make a good faith effort to obtain information in order to properly answer the special interrogatories. There is no ruling on the motion to compel further response and produce documents to Requests for Production of Documents, Set One, 21, 22, 26, 30-32, 34, 48, 49, 51-57, 59, 60-67, 69-80, 86, 96-98, 100, 101, 103, 106, 110-112, 114-116, 122-127, 132, 133, 135, 152, 155, 156, 163-173, 175-178, 180-206, and 209-214. The court finds that defendant did not meet its obligations pursuant to CCP 2031.210-240 and 2031.280 in responding to the requests. Parties are to meet and confer prior to the hearing and focus in on the response is not in compliance with CCP 2031.210-240 (including a privilege log); the objection as to vague and ambiguous and overly broad as to both time and scope is not applicable to all requests especially in a product defect/design case and whether the production is in compliance with CCP 2031.280(a) when defendant refers to other productions. Parties also to be prepared to discuss the appointment of a Discovery Referee. There is no ruling on moving party's request for sanctions. (tentative ruling continued, see next entry) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS FOIS ET AL VS. FRANCIS H. O'REILLY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Notice Of Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 28, 2021, Line 8, PLAINTIFFS DENNIS FOIS, MIRIAM FOIS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (PART 1 OF 2) Plaintiffs Miriam and Dennis Fois's summary adjudication motion is denied for three reasons. First, a summary adjudication movant bears the initial "burden of persuasion that there is no triable issue of material fact and that he is entitled to judgment as a matter of law" - a burden plaintiffs did not carry. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 850.) Plaintiffs list seven "facts" that supposedly prove Francis O'Reilly acted as a general contractor, but the list contains no legal citation or analysis. Indeed, courts hold that several acts on plaintiffs' list are not those of a general contractor: "A review of Plaintiff's duties...reveals that it was to assist, on behalf of the Owner, in coordinating the activities of the various workers to enable them to complete their assigned tasks in an organized and efficient manner, on time and on budget; to maintain records such as insurance certificates, as well as the financial books and records for the project; to keep the Owner apprised of the status of the project; to be the on-site 'point person' to respond to issues as they arose; and generally to act as the Owner's agent with respect to the various parties connected with the development of the project. Plaintiff had no responsibility or authority to perform any construction work on the project, or to enter into any contract or subcontract for the performance of such work." (The Fifth Day, LLC v. Bolotin (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 939, 948.) Second, even had plaintiffs met their initial burden (they did not), O'Reilly met his burden to show triable issues of material fact. It remains disputed whether O'Reilly acted as a general contractor or rather as a tile contractor and owner's representative or construction manager. (See id. at 947 ("licensing lawdoes not identify construction managers as workers requiring licensure"); O'Reilly Dep. 50.) Further, it remains disputed whether Rozen Home Builders was the general contractor. O'Reilly adduces evidence that Rozen obtained permits for the project naming itself as general contractor, and that Rozen performed contractor work including pouring concrete, building an upper deck, installing drywall and insulation, smoothing existing walls and ceilings, painting, framing and installing a window and door. (Higuera Dec. Exs. D-E; Ko Dec. Ex. C, No. 321.2.) (end of tentative ruling part 1 of 2, see part 2 of 2) =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS FOIS ET AL VS. FRANCIS H. O'REILLY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Dennis Fois, Miriam Fois Motion For Summary Adjudication Notice Of Motion For Summary Adjudication (For Tentative Ruling Purposes Only Part 2 Of 2)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 28, 2021, Line 8, Plaintiff Dennis Fois, Miriam Fois Motion For Summary Adjudication Notice Of Motion For Summary Adjudication (For Tentative Ruling Purposes Only Part 2 Of 2) Third, plaintiffs did not meet their burden to show they are entitled to disgorgement of the full $411,892.96 paid to O'Reilly. "The "term 'compensation' as used in [Business & Professions Code 7031(b)] denotes sums claimed as an agreed price, fee or percentage earned by performance, and also sums claimed as the reasonable value of work done under implied contract." (Ahdout v. Hekmatjah (2013) 213 Cal.App.4th 21, 32.) It remains disputed what part of the $411,892.96 was for O'Reilly's performance (and would be subject to possible disgorgement) and what amount went to pay other workers (including some hired directly by plaintiffs) or purchased appliances, doors, etc. (See O'Reilly Dep. 124-25; O'Reilly Dec. 7-8.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BEHROOZ MOHAZZABI VS. SEVERSON WERSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 28, 2021, Line 11 DEFENDANT SEVERSON WERSON, BENJAMIN HOWARD DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Plaintiff's opposition fails to address defendants' second, third, fourth and fifth demurrers, which are thus sustained without leave to amend. Because those demurrers dispose of all plaintiff's causes of action, the first demurrer need not be reached. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
